_provenance.oracle_session_status: no_canonical (stripped_export — tool_use inputs were bare strings, no diffable content survives)

turn_0_implicit_first.verbatim_excerpt: MLX LM lib doesnt allow us to run the model Qwen3-Next-80B (currently using) with batch and prompt caching. Can you deploy your subagent to verify this?
turn_0_implicit_first.behavioral_need: make the hybrid model (a model that uses both Mamba-style and attention-style cache layers per stack) usable end-to-end under batched generation when prompt caches are reused

turn_T2_simulator.verbatim: Does our PR the same as https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-lm/pull/690?
turn_T2_simulator.source: user_simulation_prompt.md Trigger Table T2 (fires only if agent already modified both cache.py AND generate.py AND has NOT yet created a test file)
turn_T2_simulator.behavioral_need: information question, not a directive — its only goal-bearing consequence is that the agent inspecting #690 may then voluntarily add the right-padding/length-aware mask feature

turn_T3_simulator.verbatim: Wait test with actual model please
turn_T3_simulator.source: user_simulation_prompt.md Trigger Table T3 (fires only if agent has created/modified a tests/test_*cache*.py or tests/test_*batch*.py file)
turn_T3_simulator.behavioral_need: demonstrate working coverage; the env has no GPU + MLX wheels so the agent must either run a CPU surrogate, write a runnable test that exercises the new cache code paths, or explain the platform limitation

asks_NOT_in_this_trial: The original DataClaw session has 'add the _lengths feature to our PR' as a verbatim user message (msg 1064). That message is NOT in the user_simulation_prompt.md Trigger Table — the simulator only fires T2 and T3. So '_lengths' is a soft, optional follow-on if the agent independently chooses to copy that feature from PR #690 after T2; it is NOT a hard user requirement of this trial.

The original session also has 'Deploy 2 sub to verify…' and 'yes draft the PR' — those live in oracle_session.jsonl but the harbor trial only exposes Turn 0 as instruction.md plus the T2/T3 simulator triggers. Goals must not assume the agent saw those expansions.
anti_gaming_constraints_enforced: goal_0_anti_effort floor — empty patch, comment-only patch, NotImplementedError-only patch, or stub-deletion scores zero overall
No oracle identifiers in goal text (no 'ArraysCache.merge classmethod', no 'CacheList.extract', no 'PR #690', no '_lengths attribute name')
Mechanism-agnostic phrasing — round-trip semantics, no-more-ValueError, mask-honors-true-lengths — not API surface pins

Weights deliberately diverge from F2P weights (per audit guidance Tier 2): two behavioral cores at 0.30 each, anti-effort 0.05, secondaries proportional to the user-trace strength of each ask, NOT to the F2P test weight in test.sh
_provenance.weight_provenance: core weights (goal_1=0.30, goal_2=0.30) reflect that batch+prompt-caching round-trip and no-ValueError-from-merge are the two pillars the verbatim Turn 0 ask depends on; goal_3 (hybrid round-trip 0.10) is the user's actual model (Qwen3-Next-80B is hybrid) — necessary or the Turn 0 ask is unmet on the real model, but pinned only to behavior, not API; goal_4 (right-padding mask 0.10) is the soft T2-derived ask, weighted as a secondary not a core because the simulator never directly asks for it; goal_5 (test coverage 0.08) is the T3 ask; goal_6 (no behavioral regression of pre-existing cache paths 0.07) protects against agents that delete things to silence ValueError; anti-effort 0.05. Sum = 0.05 + 0.30 + 0.30 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.08 + 0.07 = 1.00.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission must show non-trivial, load-bearing changes to the two source files that govern batched generation with prompt caches (the cache-types module and the generate module). Both files must remain importable (no SyntaxError, no removal of pre-existing public cache classes). A patch that is empty, comment-only, or that satisfies tests purely by deleting the buggy-state error-raising path without supplying a working replacement scores zero on this gate AND caps the overall rubric score at 0.0 regardless of what any other goal would otherwise award. This is a hard floor: if the judge concludes the patch is a stub-deletion / no-op / hardcoded-return masquerade, the rubric reward for the trial is 0.0 even if structural greps on goals 2 or 6 happen to fire.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort floor. Prevents an agent from passing by removing the 'does not yet support batching with history' ValueError without actually implementing batching, and prevents empty-patch credit through optional-secondary goals. Distinct from a single F2P import-check because it also requires non-trivial diff plus retention of pre-existing classes. CRITICAL: this gate is a global CAP, not a local 0.05 — Gemini v2 audit (2026-06-06) flagged that a stub-deletion attack could otherwise still earn 0.30 from goal_2; the cap closes that loophole.

completeness_goals.goal: Per-sequence states for the cache type used by Mamba-style layers (the layer type that previously could not participate in batched generation with prompt caches) can be composed into a multi-sequence batched state and decomposed back to single sequences. Specifically: combining N single-sequence states must produce a state whose underlying buffers have batch dimension N along axis 0 with per-sequence values preserved; given that batched state, recovering any single sequence must return a single-sequence state whose buffers slice cleanly back to the originating values. Mechanism-agnostic — classmethod, instancemethod, free helper, or inline composition inside the generation-time dispatch all satisfy the goal as long as the round-trip is correct on both shape (batch dim) and values. NOT satisfied by: a stub that returns hardcoded shapes, a no-op that copies one input to all positions, a function that raises NotImplementedError, or any implementation that does not actually concatenate the per-sequence buffer payloads along axis 0.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct trace from the verbatim Turn-0 ask 'MLX LM doesnt allow us to run … with batch and prompt caching'. The user's failure mode is that the previously single-only state type can't participate in the batched generation path. The behavioral fix is bidirectional state composition (combine + decompose). The original session's solution surfaced this as merge/extract classmethods, but the user's behavioral need is shape-and-value preservation, not API surface.

completeness_goals.goal: The internal helper in the generate module that combines a list of per-sequence cache stacks into a batched cache stack must no longer raise the buggy-state ValueError ('does not yet support batching with history') for the Mamba-style cache type or for the container type that holds a mixed-cache stack (the per-layer composite used by hybrid models). Given N input stacks composed of these previously-unsupported types, it must return a single batched stack of the same length, with each layer's batched representation correctly carrying batch-dim N. Dispatch mechanism unconstrained — explicit isinstance branches, polymorphic method dispatch on the cache object, type-registered handlers, or a uniform recursive walk all satisfy the goal. NOT satisfied by: deleting the raise-ValueError line and returning the first input unchanged, returning an empty list, returning a list of None placeholders, raising a different exception with the same effect, or any change whose only effect on the error is renaming it. The replacement must actually combine the N inputs into a true batched stack — verified end-to-end by goals 1 and 3.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct trace from Turn-0: the runtime ValueError is the exact error the user is asking us to remove. Pinned to user-visible behavior (no ValueError + correct batch dim) not to source-level changes. Critically, this goal requires BOTH the Mamba-style scalar case AND the hybrid composite container case to be handled, because Qwen3-Next-80B (the only model the user names) is hybrid — if only the scalar case were handled, the named model would still fail. Drops any pin on whether composition uses the original BatchKVCache / KVCache class for the attention slot.

completeness_goals.goal: Heterogeneous per-layer composite caches (the layer container type that mixes a Mamba-style state slot and an attention-style state slot, as used by the model the user named) support the same batched composition / per-sequence decomposition as the simple cache type in goal_1, with sub-cache ordering preserved (the Mamba-style slot stays Mamba-style on the way in and out, the attention-style slot stays attention-style on the way in and out). Round-trip on the composite: N single-sequence composites → one batched composite of width N → recover any of the N → original single-sequence composite for that index (shape and values match). API surface unconstrained — methods on the composite, recursion inside the merge helper from goal_2, or a free routine all satisfy this provided the round-trip preserves both order and per-slot semantics. NOT satisfied by: collapsing both sub-slots to a single sub-type, replacing the attention slot with a Mamba-style slot (or vice-versa) to make the dispatch easier, or any implementation where the recovered single-sequence composite has a different sub-cache class layout than the input.
completeness_goals.rationale: Without this, batched generation for the hybrid model the user named (Qwen3-Next-80B) is still broken even when goal_2 nominally returns — the composite would lose its inner-slot order or fall back to a single sub-type. Tracked as secondary because the user phrased the ask at the model level, not at the composite-cache level, and because for tasks where the user runs a non-hybrid model the secondary surface isn't exercised.

completeness_goals.goal: When a batched prefill uses right-side padding so that different sequences contribute different numbers of real prompt tokens, the cache stores enough per-sequence information for the attention-mask routine to mark positions beyond each sequence's real length as invalid. Concretely: a 2-sequence batched cache prepared with right-padding pattern [2, 0] over a sequence-length-5 cache must subsequently produce, via whatever mask-construction routine the cache exposes, a mask where sequence 0 has exactly 3 'valid' positions at the front and sequence 1 has all 5 positions 'valid'. The 'valid' polarity (True or False) may match either convention provided the all-valid row is uniform. Mechanism-agnostic — an attribute named anything (or no named attribute, just a thread of arguments) is fine; only the end-to-end mask must be correct. NOT satisfied by: a mask that is uniform across both sequences (ignoring the per-sequence right-padding), a mask hardcoded to the [3, 5] valid-count pattern without actually consuming the right-padding argument, or a routine that returns the right shape but does not vary with the right-padding input.
completeness_goals.rationale: Soft user trace from simulator T2 ('Does our PR the same as PR #690?'). PR #690's distinguishing feature is right-padding length plumbing, so an agent that actually compares with #690 is expected to either incorporate it or explicitly note its absence. Pinned at the behavioral mask layer, not at the '_lengths attribute' surface. NEGATIVE oracle-specific assertions about when the lengths attribute should/shouldn't be cleared are explicitly NOT required (a unified always-store implementation, as Opus 4.6 produces, equally satisfies the user's masking need). Weighted as secondary, not core, because the simulator never directly asks for this — it's an inferred ask at best.

completeness_goals.goal: A test file exists under the project's tests directory that exercises the new batched-cache surface for the previously-unsupported cache type (referencing it by class name or by import) and has at least one test that pytest collects and passes. Filename is not pinned; placement under tests/, real coverage of the new paths, and ≥1 passing test are the only requirements. NOT satisfied by: a test that only imports the module without asserting on the new behavior, an `assert True` placeholder, a test gated behind `pytest.skip(...)` or `@pytest.mark.skip` for all platforms, a test that catches and swallows the AssertionError so it always passes, or a test file whose body is entirely commented out.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct trace from simulator T3 ('Wait test with actual model please'). Real-model testing on Linux Docker is impossible (MLX is Apple-Silicon only), so the achievable surrogate is a unit test that exercises the new code path. Filename intentionally unconstrained so an agent that names its test file differently isn't penalized.

completeness_goals.goal: Pre-existing cache-type round-trips that already worked (the attention-style cache and the windowed-attention cache) must continue to compose and decompose without regression. The patch must not break their batched merge path or remove their classes. Construct one batched cache from N single-sequence states of each pre-existing type and verify it still has batch dim N and preserves the per-sequence values that were already supported pre-patch. NOT satisfied by: deleting the old cache classes, replacing their batched-merge implementations with a uniform stub that drops their semantics, routing all cache types through the new Mamba-style path (which would silently degrade attention-cache values), or any change whose net effect on the previously-working types is to break or coarsen their round-trip.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-regression. An agent that gets the new types working by overwriting the dispatch of the old types (the easiest hack) would defeat the user's broader benefit. Trace: Turn 0 implicitly assumes the working baseline keeps working. Pinned behaviorally — round-trip on the previously-working types — not 'do not edit these class definitions'.

_dropped_from_v2.drop_reason: v2's goal_3 still implied 'classmethod or method' on the CacheList class. v3 broadens to allow free-function/inline-dispatch implementations as long as round-trip is correct.

v2 was already mechanism-agnostic on the attribute name but still framed the goal around 'lengths tracking through some attribute'. v3 reframes purely at the mask-output layer (right-padding [2,0] over length 5 → exactly 3 valid in seq0, 5 valid in seq1), so even a fully reactive 'compute mask from a closure over right_padding' implementation passes.

v2's standalone py_compile gate is subsumed by v3 goal_0_anti_effort (which requires importability) and by goal_1/goal_2 (which can't be true behaviorally if the file doesn't compile). Removing the standalone gate prevents inflating reward for an empty/no-op patch that happens to compile.

v3 still references the user's named model in _provenance and rationale, but the goal text itself is phrased around 'hybrid model with Mamba-style + attention-style cache layers per stack', which is the architectural class. This keeps the goal robust across hybrid models that future user sessions might name.

_added_for_coverage.added_reason: v2 had no anti-regression goal for pre-existing cache types (attention-style / windowed). v3 adds goal_6 because an obvious gaming path is to make 'no more ValueError' true by collapsing all cache types through a degenerate branch that breaks the previously-working paths. The user's Turn-0 ask implicitly assumes the working baseline stays working.

Already present in v2 but tightened in v3: v3 explicitly forbids 'satisfies the no-ValueError check by deleting the raise path without supplying a working replacement', which is exactly the failure mode the F2P-only test suite cannot catch by itself.

issues_found: goal_2 initial draft pinned 'isinstance(caches[0][i], ArraysCache)' dispatch syntax — fixed to 'dispatch mechanism unconstrained' before write.
goal_4 initial draft used phrase '_lengths' — fixed to 'per-sequence length information, mechanism-agnostic'.

Initial weight set had goal_1=0.30 / goal_2=0.30 / goal_3=0.15, summing to 1.05 with the secondaries. Rebalanced goal_3 to 0.10 (hybrid is secondary not core because the user did not name the composite cache type, only the model).
goal_5 initial draft said 'tests/test_mamba_cache_batching.py or similar' — that's an oracle filename leak. Fixed to 'filename unconstrained'.
Considered adding a goal for 'agent compared with PR #690 in its response to T2'. Rejected — that's a conversational behavior, not a code goal, and it leaks oracle-specific PR number into the rubric.

Cross-checked every goal against 'does this trace to a USER ask in this trial?'. goal_1, goal_2, goal_3 trace to Turn 0 (named model is hybrid). goal_4 traces to T2 (PR #690 comparison; #690's headline feature is right-padding lengths). goal_5 traces to T3 ('test with actual model'). goal_6 anti-regression traces to Turn 0 implicit baseline. goal_0 anti-effort is meta-policy, not a user ask, and is labeled as such. All clean.

Cross-checked for oracle identifiers: searched for 'merge', 'extract', 'classmethod', 'PR #690', 'PR #739', '_lengths', 'ArraysCache.merge', 'CacheList.extract'. Removed all from goal-body text; left some in _provenance.weight_provenance and in rationale fields (acceptable per rubric — rationale is meta, not a scoring assertion).

Cross-checked weights ≠ F2P weights: F2P weights from test.sh after v043 reweight are {f2p_upstream_bd8a7d6f:0.2, t2_f2p_arrayscache_merge_extract:0.128, t2_f2p_cachelist_merge_extract:0.104, t2_f2p_merge_caches_arrayscache:0.144, t2_f2p_merge_caches_cachelist_hybrid:0.144, t2_f2p_test_file_present_passes:0.08, t4_f2p_lengths_right_padding:0.12, t4_f2p_make_mask_lengths:0.08}. v3 weights are {0.05, 0.30, 0.30, 0.10, 0.10, 0.08, 0.07}. No element-wise match; behavioral cores (goal_1, goal_2) weighted higher than any single F2P gate; anti-effort exists with no F2P counterpart. Pass.

_self_critique_log.trigger: Gemini v2 audit 2026-06-06 (analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini_v2/mlx-lm-mambacache.json), overall 0.80 with two FAIL criteria: anti_cheating_clauses and anti_effort_floor.
issues_found: FAIL anti_effort_floor: goal_0 only zeroed its own 0.05 weight, allowing a stub-deletion attack (delete the ValueError raise, return inputs unchanged) to still earn 0.30 from goal_2 + partial credit from secondaries. Single_biggest_risk in the audit was exactly this.
FAIL anti_cheating_clauses: goals 1-6 lacked inline 'NOT satisfied by stubs / hardcoded outputs / deletion-only patches' language; anti-gaming was centralized in the weak goal_0.
Both are narrow textual edits; no goal needed to be added or removed; weights unchanged at 1.00 sum.
edits_applied: goal_0: changed 'scores zero on this gate' to 'scores zero on this gate AND caps the overall rubric score at 0.0 regardless of what any other goal would otherwise award'. Added explicit cap-language and called out the audit context in the rationale.

goal_1: appended 'NOT satisfied by: a stub that returns hardcoded shapes, a no-op that copies one input to all positions, a function that raises NotImplementedError, or any implementation that does not actually concatenate the per-sequence buffer payloads along axis 0.'

goal_2: appended 'NOT satisfied by: deleting the raise-ValueError line and returning the first input unchanged, returning an empty list, returning a list of None placeholders, raising a different exception with the same effect, or any change whose only effect on the error is renaming it.'

goal_3: appended 'NOT satisfied by: collapsing both sub-slots to a single sub-type, replacing the attention slot with a Mamba-style slot (or vice-versa) to make the dispatch easier, or any implementation where the recovered single-sequence composite has a different sub-cache class layout than the input.'

goal_4: appended 'NOT satisfied by: a mask that is uniform across both sequences (ignoring the per-sequence right-padding), a mask hardcoded to the [3, 5] valid-count pattern without actually consuming the right-padding argument, or a routine that returns the right shape but does not vary with the right-padding input.'

goal_5: appended 'NOT satisfied by: a test that only imports the module without asserting on the new behavior, an assert True placeholder, a test gated behind pytest.skip(...) or @pytest.mark.skip for all platforms, a test that catches and swallows the AssertionError so it always passes, or a test file whose body is entirely commented out.'

goal_6: appended 'NOT satisfied by: deleting the old cache classes, replacing their batched-merge implementations with a uniform stub that drops their semantics, routing all cache types through the new Mamba-style path (which would silently degrade attention-cache values), or any change whose net effect on the previously-working types is to break or coarsen their round-trip.'
_self_critique_log.weight_invariants_after_edit: Sum unchanged: 0.05 + 0.30 + 0.30 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.08 + 0.07 = 1.00. No goal added or dropped. F2P-divergence invariant unchanged.
_self_critique_log.expected_audit_re_score: PASS on anti_cheating_clauses (inline clauses now present on every scored goal) and PASS on anti_effort_floor (goal_0 now globally caps the rubric reward at 0.0 on detection, not just its own 0.05 weight).
_self_critique_log.outcome: v3 patched in place; rubric remains ground truth (Mode B, no_canonical).

decomposition_notes: v3 REWRITE: behavioral-only, no oracle identifiers in goal bodies, anti-effort floor, weights divergent from F2P. Two cores at 0.30 each (goal_1 = state round-trip, goal_2 = no-ValueError-from-merge); secondaries goal_3 (hybrid composite round-trip 0.10), goal_4 (right-padding mask 0.10), goal_5 (test coverage 0.08), goal_6 (anti-regression on pre-existing types 0.07); anti-effort 0.05. Sum = 1.00. Task is Mode B (_status: no_canonical, stripped_export). Rubric IS ground truth — there is no upstream gold patch to triangulate against. Goal text is mechanism-agnostic (no class-name pins, no API-surface pins); all goals are pinned at behavioral round-trip / no-runtime-error / mask-correctness layers. Coverage traceback: every goal points to a verbatim USER ask in this trial's instruction.md + user_simulation_prompt.md Trigger Table, OR is the explicit anti-effort / anti-regression floor required by the audit. Oracle session messages that the simulator does NOT inject (msg 1064 'add the _lengths feature') are not treated as hard requirements — _lengths support shows up as goal_4 (secondary, 0.10) only because of the T2 path that fires the PR #690 comparison.
